hypothesis.statement: A single privacy-safe event contract across GA4, Yandex Metrica, and Amplitude will make the 100-visit cohort diagnosable without changing the landing conversion mechanics.
changeSet: Add a provider-neutral event contract and schema
Emit semantic funnel events to all three providers
Use stable plan and deploy identifiers
Disable Amplitude Session Replay and mask form data
Add contract tests and rebuild the browser bundle
hypothesis.expectedMetric: Server and leaderboard conversion stay comparable while client analytics expose the landing-to-registration path.

metric.name: leaderboard orders / requests
metric.denominatorSemantics: leaderboard requests in the previous eligible run

metric.name: leaderboard orders / requests
metric.denominatorSemantics: leaderboard requests in the candidate run

yandex.blocker: Observed redirect_uri_mismatch in the configured community MCP login flow; counter access and root cause remain unverified.

ga4.blocker: Admin and Data API calls returned ACCESS_TOKEN_SCOPE_INSUFFICIENT; property data were not read.

amplitude.goal: Registration Completed unique users / Landing Viewed unique users
amplitude.freshness: queried approximately one hour after the cohort; ingestion completeness not attested
amplitude.sampling: not reported by the MCP response

metric.name: Registration Completed unique users / Landing Viewed unique users

leaderboard.goal: orders / requests in the candidate run
leaderboard.freshness: collected after the documented 30-second cache window

metric.name: leaderboard orders / requests
metric.denominatorSemantics: leaderboard requests in the candidate run

discrepancies: Simulator attests 100 successful visits, while server, leaderboard, and Amplitude Landing Viewed use denominator 101.
The extra view is consistent with a technical probe but is not identity-proven and remains in the canonical denominator.
Amplitude returned independent event-set totals, not an ordered user-level funnel.
GA4 and Yandex Metrica provider data are unavailable through their configured MCPs.
processDeviations: The MCP-first requirement was clarified after the already completed redeploy and one simulator run; post-run MCP smoke is recorded instead.
GA4 raw data filename timestamp differs from its internal collectedAt; the manifest uses the internal collectedAt.
Amplitude raw JSONL has no top-level collectedAt; the immutable filename and MCP request ID provide capture-time evidence.
